title: -Resolution Urging Minnesota Governor Tim Walz to Enact an Eviction Moratorium to Prevent Displacement During the Ongoing ICE/CBP Terror Campaign

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a resolution urging Minnesota Governor Tim Walz to enact an eviction
moratorium to prevent displacement during the ongoing ICE/CBP terror campaign.
sections.background:
The Minneapolis and Saint Paul region is currently experiencing the largest immigration
operation by the Department of Homeland Security in the nation’s history, with the
Trump administration claiming to have sent over 2,000 agents from Immigration and
Customs Enforcement (ICE), Customs and Border Patrol (CBP), Homeland Security
Investigations (HSI), and other federal agencies to Minnesota. The federal
government’s campaign of discriminatory mass deportation has inflicted terror
nationwide, with the Trump administration’s fixation on Minneapolis cruelly targeting that
community with harassment, intimidation, and violence. Minnesota residents have
experienced and documented countless instances of federal agents targeting and
harassing residents based on the color of their skin, regardless of immigration status.
Federal agents have been escalating violence against legal observers and community
members protecting their neighbors, including Renée Nicole Good, who was killed by an
ICE agent on January 7, 2026, and Alex Jeffrey Pretti, who was killed by a CBP agent
on January 24, 2026. Federal tactics have included the abduction of toddlers,
attempting to trap people with offers of food assistance, and threats and attacks on
people with disabilities.
sections.current_situation:
These threatening and violent attacks on Minneapolis residents have occurred in public
spaces including schools, streets, hospitals, sidewalks, grocery stores, places of
worship, and bus stops. As a result, many residents are unable to move freely due to
the threat of harassment, violence, and abduction, forcing them to shelter-in place and
impacting their ability to work and provide for their families. These economic impacts of
the federal government's attacks on their communities have caused many families to
grapple with the fear of eviction due to an inability to safely go to work, in addition to the
ongoing threat of violence and abduction.
Page 2 of 4
On January 15, 2026, the Minneapolis City Council unanimously voted to adopt a
resolution urging Governor Walz to enact an eviction moratorium to protect residents
who have been harmed by the federal government’s unprecedented attacks and to
provide financial assistance to impacted cities to begin the significant recovery efforts.
On January 21, 2026, the Saint Paul City Council joined Minneapolis and unanimously
adopted a similar resolution. In response to the COVID-19 pandemic, Governor Walz
issued Executive Orders 20-14, 20-73, and 20-79, which imposed an eviction
moratorium and gave residents statewide a lifeline and protection during an
unprecedented national crisis. Residents in Minnesota, particularly in Minneapolis and
Saint Paul, are once again experiencing unprecedented danger and are vulnerable to
becoming displaced and homeless without government intervention, increasing their
vulnerability to violence and abduction by the Trump administration.
On January 23, 2026, the 4 x 4 Joint Task Force on Housing of the City of Berkeley
unanimously passed a resolution endorsing the calls for Governor Walz to enact an
eviction moratorium following a request from members of the Minneapolis City Council
for elected bodies around the country to support their efforts. Moreover, on January 27,
2026, the Berkeley City C
